name: Current Affairs in General Science (6): NIMHANS-2 and New Ayurveda Institutes (Budget 2026-27)

sourceRefs.label: downtoearth.org.in/theweek.in - NIMHANS-2 announcement (Budget 2026-27, 1 Feb 2026); 3 new All India Institutes of Ayurveda; WHO Global Traditional Medicine Centre, Jamnagar

researchNotes: Sixth Science current-affairs topic, opening Part A's 6th rotation lap (note: from here, Part A's topic numbering continues incrementing as a 6th lap, while Part C switches to its newly-decided rotation strategy — repeating its existing 83 topics in original order, starting today). Deliberately distinct from the already-covered Biopharma SHAKTI (Day 70) and India Semiconductor Mission (Day 66) Budget 2026-27 items. Expansion pass (Batch 18): added the AYUSH acronym breakdown for readers unfamiliar with the umbrella ministry.

reading.intro: Covers 2 Budget 2026-27 healthcare announcements: a second national mental-health institute (NIMHANS-2) and new Ayurveda/traditional-medicine infrastructure.

qa.question: What is NIMHANS-2, and when/where was it announced?
qa.answer: A second National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro Sciences, announced in the Union Budget 2026-27 (1 February 2026) by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman — proposed to be set up in NORTH India (the original NIMHANS is in Bengaluru).

qa.question: Why was North India specifically chosen for NIMHANS-2?
qa.answer: Because there are currently NO national institutes for mental healthcare in North India — NIMHANS-2 aims to bridge this geographical gap in advanced mental-health infrastructure.

qa.question: What related mental-health infrastructure upgrade was also announced in Budget 2026-27?
qa.answer: Upgrading the premier mental-health institutions at Ranchi and Tezpur into Regional Apex Institutions, alongside proposed Emergency and Trauma Care Centres in every district hospital nationwide.

qa.question: What new Ayurveda/traditional-medicine infrastructure did Budget 2026-27 propose?
qa.answer: 3 new All India Institutes of Ayurveda, upgrades to AYUSH pharmacies and Drug Testing Labs, and an upgrade to the WHO Global Traditional Medicine Centre at Jamnagar (Gujarat).

qa.question: What does AYUSH stand for, as the umbrella ministry covering India's traditional medicine systems?
qa.answer: Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy — the Ministry of AYUSH oversees research, education, and infrastructure across all five of these traditional/alternative medicine systems.
